evidence: Shivam: 'Instead of just sharing resumes, which is the common practice... we want to go up the value chain and potentially leverage career span if it can integrate into the workflow. So a customer, they get a lot more visibility than just a Resume and a LinkedIn they get this more holistic picture of what a candidate is like'

Shivam: 'Our company is building a really strong pool of engineering talent and teams across India... it's a full captive center that the US company owns and is operating... Harvey AI Anthropic OpenAI are building teams in India. And so we're building teams in that structure.'

Shivam: 'People say outsourcing, but it's a full captive center that the US company owns and is operating... it's something called capability centers... not contracting a vendor that is managing the resources. It's actually your embedded team.'
V: 'More so the candidate side... the company side is like great. But if we don't have to deal with it, we really would rather not. We would rather just help.'

V: 'The ability to advertise it matters the most... we're trying to sell this sucker. Or at least we're trying to like get Ilsa and I sold... we're basically not optimizing for revenue. So there's flexibility as long as we can sort of cover our bases and advertise the [expletive] out of it.'
V: 'I'm really hoping that we can help your time to place it down because I'm assuming that will be a big competitive advantage like being able to produce names within a day... I'm sure will help.'
Ilse: 'When we get a job description... we break it down into like 10 responsibilities, 10 soft skills, 10 hard skills and we have like a paragraph of assessment criteria for each one of those.'

Ilse: 'There's frequently skills that don't actually attach to responsibilities, which is in my opinion a sign that you don't actually need that responsibility. If it's not tied to a responsibility... it's just more wish lists of employers.'
Meeting context: Shivam presenting CorridorX workflow and integration plans

Ilse: 'People hiring in America have expectations that you can talk about yourself like this... whether or not it's necessary for you to do your job is up for debate. But it's unfortunately necessary in the US job market. And I think that's a really powerful thing as well to sell people that like it's practice.'
V: 'I have largely finalized the one pagers... These are just the positioning documents. These are the overall argument that we're making and the sort of like proof points that we'll be using.'

Ilse: 'I already have a credit based system but I gave him 100 credits. There's currently nothing to stop them from doing all hundred of them then and there... I'm adding in additional limitations for employers that limits the number of scan requests they can have at once.'
Ilse: 'What you and Logan described to me was you would be charging them 50 to $100 per scan. So I just ran with that.'
V: 'That should be theoretically a really good case study because they want expedience over all else so they'll be pretty much like pushing folks to use it.'
V: 'We need to scale the outbound.' N: 'Agreed. Let's hire more SDRs.' V: 'I'll talk to the board.'
